channel.title: Signal & Noise
channel.description: Weekly AI-generated analysis at the intersection of media and artificial intelligence. Script written by Claude. Voice by ElevenLabs. Published every Friday.
channel.itunes:author: Nate Kelly

item.title: Ep. 4: When AI Eats the News That Feeds It
item.description: Click-through rates from AI chatbots hover around zero-point-three-three percent. Traditional Google Search? Eight-point-six percent. That is a twenty-six-fold difference — and it's the single number every news publisher in America should have tattooed on the back of their hand right now. Because th...

item.title: Ep. 3: When the Traffic Disappears Who Survives
item.description: Earlier this year, the Washington Post announced it would cut roughly thirty percent of its workforce, citing AI as part of the reason. Let that land for a second. One of the most storied newsrooms in American journalism, the paper that broke Watergate, is slashing nearly a third of its staff, and a...

item.title: Ep. 1: The Newsroom That Fired Its Memory
item.description: Three weeks ago, the Philadelphia Inquirer quietly laid off its entire archive research team. Twelve people, most of them with more than a decade at the paper, replaced by an internal retrieval system built on top of a fine-tuned large language model. The system can surface any story from 1829 to ye...
